Title: Total biosynthesis of the medicinal triterpenoid saponin astragalosides

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pESC-URA/galactose-inducible yeast expression; pEAQ-HT transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana (promoter not specified in excerpt)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Use AmOSC3 as the entry-point committed-step backbone synthase for astragaloside engineering, but stack it with the six tailoring enzymes and preferably precursor-supply enhancement such as NbtHMGR overexpression. Because AmOSC3 also likely contributes to sterol biosynthesis and is not root-specific, pathway balancing is needed to avoid competition with endogenous sterol metabolism.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the cycloartenol scaffold for astragaloside biosynthesis; enables heterologous astragaloside IV production only when combined with downstream tailoring enzymes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae CC-4 with GC-MS detection of cycloartenol; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with pathway reconstitution.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Roots are the native accumulation site of astragalosides; transient expression assayed in N. benthamiana leaves under 16 h light/8 h dark at 25 °C.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: No apparent root-specific expression in A. membranaceus root according to transcript data.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: No apparent root-specific expression in A. membranaceus root.
Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: AmOSC3/AmCAS1; specific accession not provided in excerpt
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Chromosome-level genome assembly, RNA-seq expression profiling; genome project accession PRJNA917072
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also described as cycloartenol synthase 1. The AmOSC3-containing chromosome 8 cluster with evm.model.LG08.328 and evm.model.LG08.329 was excluded from astragaloside biosynthesis because neighboring genes lacked expression/activity. AmOSC3 is physically separate from the chromosome 1 tailoring-enzyme BGC.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential flux competition with sterol biosynthesis; engineering may require precursor boosting and balancing to avoid growth effects.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AmOSC3 catalyses conversion of 2,3-oxidosqualene to cycloartenol, the first committed step toward astragalosides. 2. It enabled construction of the cycloartenol-producing yeast strain CC-4 and supported full pathway reconstitution in N. benthamiana. 3. Unlike the six tailoring genes, AmOSC3 is not located within the chromosome 1 astragaloside tailoring BGC and may also serve sterol biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal and nutraceutical compound; immunomodulatory, autophagy modulator, antiviral veterinary application

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Yeast expression in pESC-derived system; transient expression in pEAQ-HT in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Astragalus membranaceus; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential early tailoring enzyme for astragaloside production. Should be stacked with AmCYP88D7, AmCYP71D756, AmOGD1, AmGT11 and AmGT36, plus AmOSC3 and CPR; useful target for pathway reconstruction and marker-assisted identification of high-AG genotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cycloartenol at C-16 to form 16-hydroxycycloartenol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates oxidation tailoring of the cycloartenol scaffold and is indispensable for downstream cycloastragenol/astragaloside IV formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast in vivo expression with HPLC product detection and structure identification of product 6; RNAi hairy-root silencing causing reduced astragalosides; N. benthamiana pathway reconstitution.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an early dedicated intermediate/precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root-predominant native expression; assays in yeast and N. benthamiana leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream tailoring step after scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and RNA-seq expression profiling; PRJNA917072
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mapped near AmGT11 in the chromosome 1 BGC. Discussion text once mentions AmCYP88D5, likely a typo for AmCYP88D25.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces astragaloside accumulation; no specific growth penalty reported.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CYP88 family cytochrome P450, CYP85 clan
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed early oxidation step; key control point implied but not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Microsomal/endoplasmic-reticulum-associated (inferred for plant CYP450; microsomal assays used)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AmCYP88D25 was the only root-expressed CYP among 15 tested that converted cycloartenol to a new product. 2. The product was identified as 16-hydroxycycloartenol, establishing C-16 hydroxylation. 3. RNAi knockdown reduced astragalosides and pathway reconstitution confirmed its essential upstream role.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal immunomodulatory and antiviral bioactive saponin

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: In vitro recombinant enzyme assay after E. coli expression; transient expression in pEAQ-HT in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred 3-O-xylosyltransferase for engineering astragalosides because of root-specific expression and strong preference for UDP-xylose. Can be stacked with AmGT36 to complete final disaccharide assembly through parallel routes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose preferentially to the 3-OH of cycloastragenol to form astramembrannin II; also glucosylates brachyoside B to astragaloside IV, showing substrate promiscuity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Drives the principal 3-O-xylosylation step and contributes to final astragaloside IV formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogeny-guided candidate selection; in vitro UGT assays with UDP-Xyl/UDP-Glc and LC-MS; kinetic analysis; RNAi hairy-root knockdown; N. benthamiana pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product; installs one of final sugar moieties
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root-predominant expression in native plant; tested in vitro and in N. benthamiana leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly, RNA-seq, phylogenetic analysis; PRJNA917072
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous to AmmUGT15 from A. membranaceus var. mongholicus. Accepts both cycloastragenol and brachyoside B, enabling parallel final glycosylation routes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces astragalosides; alone in N. benthamiana accumulates intermediate 5 rather than final product unless AmGT36 is also present.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Astramembrannin II (5) from cycloastragenol; Astragaloside IV (1) from brachyoside B (4)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Cycloastragenol (3) with UDP-Xyl preferred; also brachyoside B (4) with UDP-Glc
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal tailoring determinant; not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AmGT11 catalyses 3-O-xylosylation of cycloastragenol and prefers UDP-xylose over UDP-glucose. 2. It also glucosylates brachyoside B to astragaloside IV, showing substrate promiscuity with AmGT36. 3. RNAi and transient-expression studies confirmed its importance in planta for astragaloside accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal immunomodulatory and antiviral bioactive saponin

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: In vitro recombinant enzyme assay after E. coli expression; transient expression in pEAQ-HT in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required partner with AmGT11 for efficient astragaloside IV assembly. Suitable for metabolic engineering to complete 6-O-glucosylation and exploit parallel-route flexibility.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to the 6-OH of cycloastragenol-derived substrates, converting cycloastragenol to astraverrucin I/brachyoside B route products and converting astramembrannin II to astragaloside IV; effectively performs 6-O-glucosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes the second glycosylation needed for astragaloside IV and supports parallel-route final product formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assays with purified protein and LC-MS; kinetic analysis showing preference for compound 5; RNAi hairy-root knockdown causing reduced AGs and isoastragaloside IV accumulation; N. benthamiana reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product; installs one of final sugar moieties
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assayed in vitro and in N. benthamiana leaves; native expression associated with root-active BGC.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in the root-associated BGC region; exact tissue profile not detailed beyond cluster expression heatmap.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in A. membranaceus root-associated BGC region.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: OQ365045? not correct for AmGT36; paper states GenBank OQ365041–OQ365045 include AmGT36 but exact mapping not individually specified
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly, RNA-seq, phylogenetic analysis; PRJNA917072
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: UGT93 family member; 97% amino acid identity with AmGT37, but only AmGT36 was successfully cloned. Exhibits substrate promiscuity and prefers compound 5 kinetically.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduced AGs and caused accumulation of isoastragaloside IV; engineering without complementary UGT can stall intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Brachyoside B (4) from cycloastragenol; Astragaloside IV (1) from astramembrannin II (5)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Cycloastragenol (3) and astramembrannin II (5) with UDP-Glc
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal tailoring determinant; not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AmGT36 catalyses 6-O-glucosylation on cycloastragenol-derived substrates. 2. It can convert cycloastragenol to brachyoside B and astramembrannin II to astragaloside IV, demonstrating pathway parallelism with AmGT11. 3. RNAi and transient expression validated its role in planta, with knockdown causing reduced AGs and isoastragaloside IV accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal immunomodulatory and antiviral bioactive saponin

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Yeast expression in pESC-derived system; transient expression in pEAQ-HT in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Astragalus membranaceus; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary mid-pathway oxidase for 6-hydroxylation branch. Should be co-expressed with AmCYP88D25 and AmCYP71D756 because pathway flux proceeds through cooperative oxidation toward compound 12.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates 16-hydroxycycloartenol at C-6 to form 6,16-dihydroxycycloartenol and can also convert epoxide intermediate 10 to 12.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces the C-6 hydroxylation required for cycloastragenol formation; acts cooperatively with AmCYP71D756.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression in yeast with AmCYP88D25 followed by HPLC/structural identification of product 8; microsomal in vitro assays showing 10 to 12 conversion; RNAi hairy-root silencing; N. benthamiana transient expression.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for later ring-forming oxidation steps
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root-associated expression; assays in yeast, microsomes, and N. benthamiana leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Part of root-expressed chromosome 1 BGC in A. membranaceus.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and RNA-seq expression profiling; PRJNA917072
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to CYP88 family. No activity toward cycloartenol alone in yeast, indicating substrate specificity for hydroxylated intermediate 6.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces AG production; no explicit developmental penalty reported.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CYP88 family cytochrome P450, CYP85 clan
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key cooperative midstream oxidation step, not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AmCYP88D7 does not act on cycloartenol directly but hydroxylates the AmCYP88D25 product 6 at C-6. 2. It also converts epoxide intermediate 10 to 12, acting interchangeably with AmCYP71D756 order. 3. Functional assays and RNAi support its essential contribution to cycloastragenol formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal immunomodulatory and antiviral bioactive saponin

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Yeast expression in pESC-derived system; transient expression in pEAQ-HT in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Astragalus membranaceus; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential epoxidase in pathway reconstruction. Must be combined with AmCYP88D25, AmCYP88D7, and AmOGD1 to form cycloastragenol; useful for accessing cycloartane epoxide intermediates and analog engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Epoxidizes the 24,25-double bond of 16-hydroxycycloartenol to form 24,25-epoxy-16-hydroxycycloartenol; also acts on 8 to produce 12. In heterologous hosts, the epoxide is often hydrolysed by endogenous epoxide hydrolases to vicinal diols.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the 24,25-epoxide necessary for later C-20 oxidation and spontaneous tetrahydrofuran ring formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast co-expression yielding dihydroxylated shunt product 9; microsomal in vitro conversion of 6 to 10 and 8 to 12; transient expression in N. benthamiana yielding hydrolysed product 11; RNAi hairy-root silencing causing reduced AGs and accumulation of intermediate 8.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor required for THF-ring-containing aglycone production
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Host-dependent epoxide hydrolysis occurred in yeast and N. benthamiana due to endogenous epoxide hydrolases.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Part of root-expressed chromosome 1 BGC in A. membranaceus.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and RNA-seq expression profiling; PRJNA917072
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to CYP71 family. Apparent diol products 9 and 11 are host-derived hydrolysis artifacts of epoxide products 10 and 12.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing caused intermediate accumulation (8) and lowered AGs; heterologous hosts may generate side products via endogenous epoxide hydrolases.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CYP71 family cytochrome P450, CYP71 clan
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key cooperative midstream oxidation step, not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AmCYP71D756 catalyses 24,25-epoxidation on the cycloartane side chain. 2. Host epoxide hydrolases convert its products to diagnostic diols, helping infer the true epoxide intermediates. 3. RNAi and heterologous assays confirm that this epoxidation step is required for cycloastragenol and astragaloside IV biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal immunomodulatory and antiviral bioactive saponin

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: In vitro recombinant enzyme assay after E. coli expression; transient expression in pEAQ-HT in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical late oxidation enzyme for producing cycloastragenol aglycone. Combine with upstream CYPs and downstream UGTs for full pathway engineering; because THF ring closure is spontaneous after oxidation, providing this enzyme may be sufficient for aglycone formation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses C-20 hydroxylation of 24,25-epoxy-6,16-dihydroxycycloartenol (12) to generate a transient triol epoxide intermediate (13), which then cyclizes non-enzymatically to cycloastragenol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Drives formation of the cycloastragenol aglycone by enabling THF-ring formation through C-20 oxidation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assays with purified protein; substrate screening and kinetics identifying 12 as favored substrate; product detection of cycloastragenol; RNAi hairy-root silencing lowering AGs; pathway reconstitution in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target aglycone precursor and enables final ring closure toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root-specific expression; Fe2+, 2-oxoglutarate and ascorbate-dependent in vitro assays; spontaneous cyclization inferred chemically.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High root-specific expression in A. membranaceus.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High root-specific expression in A. membranaceus.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: THF ring formation after C-20 hydroxylation appears non-enzymatic based on chemical model reactions with protopanaxatriol epoxidation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces AG accumulation; no specific developmental penalty reported.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Cycloastragenol (3) via transient C-20-hydroxylated epoxide intermediate 13
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Late key control step for aglycone formation; favored substrate kinetics support a decisive role but rate limitation not formally tested
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AmOGD1 is a root-expressed 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase within the chromosome 1 BGC. 2. It preferentially oxidizes compound 12 at C-20, leading to cycloastragenol formation. 3. The subsequent tetrahydrofuran ring appears to form spontaneously after AmOGD1 action, completing the aglycone biosynthetic logic.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal immunomodulatory and antiviral bioactive saponin

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: In vitro recombinant enzyme assay after E. coli expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible alternative 3-O-glycosyltransferase for generating glucose-conjugated analogs rather than the main astragaloside IV route; less suitable than AmGT11 for authentic AG IV engineering because it prefers UDP-glucose.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses 3-O-glycosylation of cycloastragenol using UDP-Glc preferentially, and can also use UDP-Xyl to form the xyloside product.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms glycosylated cycloastragenol derivatives in vitro but is less likely to be the main enzyme for natural AG IV accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic selection and in vitro enzyme assays with UDP-Glc and UDP-Xyl; kinetic comparison.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Alternative glycosylation route/analogue formation rather than main target-product route
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root-predominant expression noted among candidates, but main physiological relevance considered lower than AmGT11.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows opposite sugar-donor preference to AmGT11 and resembles AmmUGT14 from A. membranaceus var. mongholicus.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert flux to non-major glucosylated derivatives instead of authentic astragaloside IV.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Astraverrucin I (7) and/or astramembrannin II (5) depending on donor sugar
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AmGT72 glycosylates cycloastragenol at 3-OH in vitro. 2. It prefers UDP-glucose, unlike AmGT11 which prefers UDP-xylose. 3. Because natural astragalosides are mainly 3-O-xylosylated in roots, AmGT72 is considered a less likely major physiological enzyme for AG IV biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful metabolic engineering lever to boost precursor supply and substantially increase astragaloside IV titre in heterologous hosts. Best used with complete astragaloside pathway rather than alone. Monitor potential pleiotropic effects from mevalonate-pathway overactivation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased astragaloside IV production in engineered N. benthamiana from 0.293 to 2.224 mg g−1 dry weight.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression with the full astragaloside pathway in N. benthamiana followed by metabolite quantification.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong precursor-supply enhancer for heterologous AG IV production, though not a native dedicated astragaloside regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves grown under 16 h light/8 h dark at 25 °C.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Native to N. benthamiana; expression pattern not described
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Precursor supply through the mevalonate/isoprenoid pathway
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as truncated 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl-coenzyme A reductase gene from N. benthamiana. Acts as an upstream pathway booster rather than a specific astragaloside BGC regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible broad metabolic perturbation from elevated isoprenoid flux; no growth penalty reported in transient assay.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Increases upstream precursor availability for AmOSC3-dependent triterpene formation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpressing NbtHMGR with the complete astragaloside pathway greatly boosted AG IV accumulation in N. benthamiana. 2. The effect indicates precursor supply from the mevalonate pathway limits heterologous production. 3. NbtHMGR is therefore a practical engineering booster, not a dedicated native regulatory gene of the A. membranaceus pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal immunomodulatory and antiviral bioactive saponin
